steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank introduces the noun 'advocate,' part of the subject, immediately followed by the proper noun 'Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez' — a restrictive appositive that identifies which lawyer and cycling advocate is meant. A restrictive appositive takes no commas because removing the name would leave the subject unidentified.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘advocate Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez,’: trailing comma only; unmatched and incorrectly inserts a boundary before the verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘advocate, Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez,’: commas on both sides signal a nonrestrictive appositive, implying the name is optional extra information — it is not. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘advocate Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez’: no commas; the name directly and necessarily identifies the subject. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘advocate, Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez’: opening comma only; unmatched and signals a nonrestrictive open without a proper close. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
